title: Decree implementing the rules of road traffic

parts.OZNAČOVÁNÍ OSOB, VĚCÍ A VOZIDEL VE ZVLÁŠ: MARKING OF PERSONS, OBJECTS AND VEHICLES IN SPECIAL CASES
parts.VÝJIMKY Z OMEZENÍ JÍZDY NĚKTERÝCH VOZIDE: EXCEPTIONS FROM RESTRICTIONS ON DRIVING OF CERTAIN VEHICLES

p1.heading: Design of vertical traffic signs
p1.text:
(1) Permanent traffic signs shall be affixed to posts, buildings or other structures firmly connected to the ground.

(2) The red and white stripes on the post (stand) on which a portable traffic sign is placed shall be 100 to 200 mm wide and retroreflective.

(3) Variable message signs are signs whose design may change. The colour scheme of variable message signs may differ from permanent traffic signs, in that the background of variable message signs is dark and the inscriptions, symbols and borders are light.

(4) To emphasize the meaning and improve visibility, a vertical traffic sign may be placed on a retroreflective yellow-green fluorescent background. Such a background cannot be used in combination with light signals.

p2.heading: Placement of vertical traffic signs
p2.text:
(1) Vertical traffic signs shall be placed so as to be visible in time and from a sufficient distance to road users for whom they are intended.

(2) Vertical traffic signs shall be placed at the right edge of the road or above the roadway; to emphasize their meaning, signs located at the right edge of the road may also be repeated at the left edge of the road. If expedient, or if stipulated in the appendix to this decree, informative directional signs may also be placed elsewhere than at the right edge of the road.

(3) Vertical traffic signs „Pedestrian path“, „End of pedestrian path“, „Cycle path“, „End of cycle path“, „Shared pedestrian and cycle path“, „End of shared pedestrian and cycle path“, „Divided pedestrian and cycle path“, „End of divided pedestrian and cycle path“, „Bridleway“, „End of bridleway“ and vertical zone traffic signs (end of zone) may, if appropriate, be placed only on the left side of the road on the reverse side of another of the aforementioned traffic signs.

(4) Signs regulating stopping or standing shall be placed at the edge of the roadway or road to which they relate.

(5) Permanent signs and their structures must not encroach upon the defined part of the traffic space designated for vehicle traffic.

(6) If it is necessary, in the interest of road traffic safety, to give advance warning of a place with a restriction, prohibition or command from a greater distance, the corresponding vertical traffic sign supplemented by an additional panel „Distance“ shall be used; for marking a place on a diverging road, an additional panel „Directional arrow“ (hereinafter referred to as „advance sign“) shall be used.

(7) A vertical traffic sign implying an obligation to stop a vehicle must be placed so that the driver, taking into account local conditions, is able to stop the vehicle safely. If this requirement cannot be met, an advance sign or other suitable sign shall also be used.

(8) Posts or stands of portable traffic signs may, in justified cases, be exceptionally placed on the roadway.

(9) Nothing that is not related to the traffic sign, traffic facility or traffic information device may be marked or placed on traffic signs and their posts, stands or structures.

p3.heading: Validity of vertical traffic signs
p3.text:
(1) A vertical traffic sign placed next to the road is valid for the entire road in the given direction of travel, with the exception of a sign regulating stopping or standing, which is valid only for the side of the road where it is placed.

(2) A vertical traffic sign placed above the roadway is valid for the entire roadway in the given direction of travel. However, if a prohibitory or mandatory sign is placed above a marked lane, it is valid only for the lane above which it is placed.

(3) A prohibition, restriction or command is terminated by the nearest intersection or by the relevant traffic sign.

(4) The validity of a prohibition, restriction or command expressed by a portable traffic sign placed on a vehicle, which marks a mobile workplace consisting of a slow-moving vehicle or group of vehicles, ends 100 m in the direction of travel behind this vehicle or behind the last vehicle of the group, if it is a group of vehicles.

(5) Priority signs „Intersection with a minor road“, „Main road“, „End of main road“, „Give way!“, „Give way to tram!“ and „Stop, give way!“ are valid for the nearest intersection.

(6) The validity of a zone traffic sign is terminated by a sign informing of its end, unless otherwise stipulated. The traffic sign „Pedestrian path“, „Cycle path“, „Shared pedestrian and cycle path“, „Divided pedestrian and cycle path“ or „Bridleway“ terminates the validity of another of these traffic signs.

(7) The validity of informative signs indicating parking lots ends 5 m before the boundary of the nearest intersection, unless the manner and arrangement of permitted standing is indicated by horizontal traffic signs or unless the validity of informative signs indicating parking lots is terminated in another way. Entry to or exit from places located outside the road interrupts the validity of these traffic signs.

(8) The invalidity of a traffic sign or its part, which results from a temporary traffic regulation on roads, may be expressed by

- a) striking through with an orange-black stripe (stripes) or covering with a red cross; however, the invalidity of a vertical traffic sign regulating priority must not be expressed in this way, or

- b) other means, for example, by covering or dismantling the active surface of the sign.

Section 2

Special provisions on vertical traffic signs and their classification

p4.heading: Warning Signs
p4.text:
(1) Warning signs are usually placed before the designated location outside a municipality at a distance of 100 to 250 m, and within a municipality at 50 to 100 m. If, for serious reasons, it is not possible to adhere to the specified distance range, the warning traffic sign shall be supplemented with an additional sign „Distance“ indicating the actual distance to the designated location. On a cycle path and a pedestrian and cycle path, this distance may be shortened.

(2) The design, meaning, and, where applicable, use of warning signs are set out in Annex No. 1 to this Decree.

p5.heading: Priority Signs
p5.text: The design, meaning, and, where applicable, use of priority signs are set out in Annex No. 2 to this Decree.

p6.heading: Prohibitory Signs
p6.text:
(1) Prohibitory signs are placed at the point from which the prohibition or restriction applies, or where it ends. The sign may be repeated during the period of validity of the prohibition or restriction to emphasize it.

(2) The design, meaning, and, where applicable, use of prohibitory signs are set out in Annex No. 3 to this Decree.

p7.heading: Mandatory Signs
p7.text:
(1) Mandatory signs are placed at the point where the command begins, or where it ends.

(2) The design, meaning, and, where applicable, use of mandatory signs are set out in Annex No. 4 to this Decree.

p8.heading: Informative Signs
p8.text:
(1) Informative signs are divided into zone informative signs, operational informative signs, directional informative signs, and other informative signs.

(2) Zone informative signs indicate the beginning or end of an area in which the relevant provisions of the rules of road traffic resulting from a legal regulation apply.

(3) The color design of directional informative signs may differ from their depiction according to this Decree depending on the type of destination or the category of the road to which the signs lead or on which they are located. For motorways, a green background is used; for other roads, a blue or white background is used.

(4) The design, meaning, and, where applicable, use of informative signs are set out in Annex No. 5 to this Decree.

p9.heading: Additional Panels
p9.text: The design, meaning, and, where applicable, use of additional panels are set out in Annex No. 6 to this Decree.

p10.heading: Designated Symbols
p10.text:
(1) On vertical traffic signs, a designated symbol may be used to supplement, specify, or replace textual information. On informative signs, additional panels, and devices for operational information, other suitable generally understandable symbols may also be used.

(2) The design, meaning, and, where applicable, use of designated symbols are set out in Annex No. 7 to this Decree.

Part 2

Horizontal Traffic Signs

p11.heading: General Provisions for Horizontal Traffic Signs
p11.text:
(1) Horizontal traffic signs are marked on the roadway or other paved part of the road.

(2) Temporary horizontal traffic signs indicating lines or arrows or marking pedestrian crossings or cycle lanes are executed in yellow or orange. They may also be executed in the form of an outline of the respective horizontal traffic sign.

(3) Temporary invalidity of horizontal traffic signs is indicated by crossing them out with yellow or orange lines.

p12.heading: Classification of Horizontal Traffic Signs
p12.text:
(1) Horizontal traffic signs are divided into longitudinal lines, transverse lines, arrows, markings for stopping and parking, markings for stops and prohibitions of stopping and parking, and other horizontal traffic signs.

(2) Markings for stopping and parking are used to indicate the arrangement of stopping and parking and, where applicable, to determine the method of parking in relation to the edge of the road or the arrangement of the parking area.

(3) The design, meaning, and, where applicable, use of horizontal traffic signs are set out in Annex No. 8 to this Decree.

p13.heading: Light Signals
p13.text:
(1) Light signals for pedestrians must be supplemented by a corresponding acoustic signal.

(2) To emphasize the meaning and improve visibility, light signals may be highlighted by a white frame.

(3) The types, design, meaning, and, where applicable, use of light signals are set out in Annex No. 9 to this Decree.

p14.heading: Acoustic Signals
p14.text:
(1) Acoustic signals inform visually impaired persons about the current light signal at intersections and pedestrian crossings controlled by light signals. At railway-road crossings, acoustic signals inform about the current light signal of two alternately flashing red lights placed side by side. Acoustic signals may also be controlled by a remote transmitter for visually impaired persons.

(2) Acoustic signals at pedestrian crossings have a ticking sound or short tones with a frequency of 450 to 550 Hz.

(3) The acoustic signaling device must enable a visually impaired person approaching the location (pole) with acoustic signaling to safely distinguish whether the pedestrian signal in their direction of travel indicates „Stop!“ or „Go“.

(4) „Pedestrian signal with Stop! indication“, „Pedestrian and cyclist signal with Stop! indication“ and „Signal of two alternately flashing red lights placed side by side“ are expressed by an acoustic signal. The red light signal phase is indicated by an acoustic signal with a frequency of 1.5 Hz (1.5 beats per second). At a light railway crossing safety device, the sound of the acoustic signal is one octave higher.

(5) „Pedestrian signal with Go indication“ and „Pedestrian and cyclist signal with Go indication“ are expressed by an acoustic signal. The green light signal phase is indicated by an acoustic signal with a frequency of 8 Hz (8 beats per second). At a light railway crossing safety device, the sound of the acoustic signal is one octave higher.

p15.text:
(1) Traffic facilities include:

- a) traffic facilities listed in Annex No. 10 to this Decree,

- b) reflectors, which are placed in orange on the right and in white on the left in the direction of travel,

- c) reflective mirror,

- d) railings, crash barriers, guide walls, guide thresholds, guide kerbs,

- e) traffic safety devices, which include in particular impact attenuators and devices for traffic safety information.

(2) The provisions on the placement of vertical traffic signs shall apply to the placement of traffic facilities. Traffic facilities may also be placed on the left edge of the road.

(3) The types, design, meaning, or use of certain traffic facilities are listed in Annex No. 10 to this Decree.

p16.text:
(1) The provisions on the placement of vertical traffic signs shall apply to the placement of operational information devices. Operational information devices may also be placed on the left edge of the road.

(2) The types, design, meaning, or use of operational information devices are listed in Annex No. 11 to this Decree.

p17.heading: Application for an exemption from restrictions on the driving of certain vehicles
p17.text:
An application for an exemption from restrictions on the driving of certain vehicles shall contain:

- a) the name, surname, and usual place of residence of the natural person or, if the applicant is a legal entity, its business name (name), identification number (if assigned), and registered office,

- b) the territorial scope, or the route of vehicle movement, and the period of the requested exemption,

- c) the purpose and justification for the exemption,

- d) the license plate number, type, make, and model of the vehicle to which the exemption is to apply,

- e) the name, surname, and permanent residence or business name (name), identification number (if assigned), and registered office of the vehicle operator, if not the applicant himself,

- f) the signature of the applicant, or the imprint of their stamp.

p18.heading: Details on the marking of obstacles to traffic on roads
p18.text:
(1) The method and extent of marking an obstacle to traffic on a road shall be chosen by its originator, or by the owner or administrator of the road, taking into account the position and nature of the obstacle, the degree of potential danger, and the traffic significance of the affected road.

(2) A police officer or municipal police officer may, for the time strictly necessary to ensure traffic safety, draw attention to an obstacle to traffic on a road with a vehicle equipped with a special blue warning light, or by other suitable means.

p19.heading: Pattern and method of use of clothing accessories with retroreflective material marking
p19.text:
(1) Clothing accessories are a fluorescent yellow or fluorescent orange-red blouse, coat, t-shirt, vest, or trousers supplemented with retroreflective strips (hereinafter referred to as „warning clothing“). Retroreflective strips and fluorescent surfaces must be suitably placed and executed in a sufficient area on the warning clothing. These conditions are met if the warning clothing complies with the requirements of the relevant harmonized European standard^1).

(2) Patterns of warning clothing are depicted in Annex No. 12 to this Decree.

p20.heading: Marking of persons authorized to stop vehicles
p20.text:
(1) A person performing work related to the administration, maintenance, measurement, repairs, and construction of a road or to ensuring traffic safety on roads, if these activities are carried out while maintaining traffic on the affected part of the road, must wear warning clothing.

(2) A person authorized by the municipal office of a municipality with extended powers to ensure the safe crossing of children and school youth across a road in the vicinity of a school facility (hereinafter referred to as „authorized person“) must wear warning clothing „Marking of an authorized person ensuring safe crossing of children and school youth“ or „Vest“.

(3) A person listed in the permit for special use of a road consisting of organizing a sports, cultural, religious, entertainment, or similar event according to the Road Act must wear warning clothing.

(4) A person listed in the permit for special use according to the Road Act as an escort for the transport of particularly heavy or oversized loads must wear warning clothing.

p21.heading: Special marking of vehicles and persons
p21.text:
(1) Special marking of vehicles, depiction, and meaning are listed in Annex No. 13 to this Decree.

(2) The special marking of a blind person is a white cane; the marking of a deafblind person is a cane with white and red stripes 100 mm wide. Both the white cane and the cane with white and red stripes must be equipped with at least one reflective white stripe 50 mm wide, and at least a continuous third of its length must have a diameter of at least 12 mm.

(3) The marking of a bus transporting children must have dimensions of at least 400 × 400 mm and must be visibly placed on both the front and rear ends of the bus when transporting children, without obstructing the driver's view. Inverse color display via electronic information panels with an active area of at least 310 × 180 mm is also permissible.

p22.heading: Marking of seats equipped with a restraint system
p22.text:
(1) In a vehicle of category M2 or M3^2) registered in the Czech Republic, which is equipped with a safety restraint system, a clearly displayed pictogram must be visibly placed for each seat, which may be supplemented in the upper part with the text „FASTEN YOUR SEATBELT“.

(2) The basic area of the pictogram is a blue circle and a figure symbol, which is executed in white. The diameter of the pictogram circle must not be less than 7.5 cm. The pictogram pattern is listed in Annex No. 13 to this Decree.

p23.heading: Instructions for traffic control on roads
p23.text:
(1) Instructions of a police officer when controlling traffic on roads are:

- a) „Stop!“,

- b) „Attention!“,

- c) „Clear!“,

- d) „Speed up!“,

- e) „Slow down!“.

(2) The pattern of police officer instructions for traffic control on roads is listed in Annex No. 14 to this Decree.

p24.heading: Equipment and marking of a member of the military police service
p24.text:
(1) A member of the military traffic service shall, when directing traffic on roads, be dressed in a military uniform supplemented by a yellow high-visibility vest with two horizontal and two vertical retroreflective strips at least 50 mm wide. The design of the vest must comply with a special technical regulation^1).

(2) When directing traffic on roads, a member of the military traffic service shall use the traffic device “Directional arrow” or “Stop paddle for police officers”.

p25.heading: Authorization of a member of the military traffic service to direct traffic on roads
p25.text:
(1) The authorization of a member of the military traffic service to direct traffic on roads shall contain

- a) the name, surname, and position of the person who issued the authorization to the member,

- b) the rank, name, and surname of the member of the military traffic service authorized to direct traffic on roads,

- c) the designation of the place or section of the road where the member of the military traffic service is authorized to direct traffic on roads,

- d) the date and time for which the member of the military traffic service is authorized to direct traffic on roads,

- e) the specific task for directing traffic on roads,

- f) the period of validity of the member's authorization,

- g) the date of issue, the signature of the person who issued the member's authorization, and the impression of the stamp.

(2) The template for the authorization of a member of the military traffic service to direct traffic on roads is provided in Annex No. 15 to this decree.

p26.heading: Authorization of a person to ensure the safe crossing of children and school youth
p26.text:
(1) The authorization of a person to ensure the safe crossing of children and school youth across a road near a school facility, issued by the municipal office of a municipality with extended powers, shall contain

- a) the name and surname, date and place of birth of the authorized person,

- b) the place on the road where the authorized person is entitled to ensure the safe crossing of children and school youth across the road,

- c) the time during which the authorized person is entitled to ensure the safe crossing of children and school youth across the road,

- d) the period of validity of the authorization from the municipal office of the municipality with extended powers,

- e) the name and surname, position, and signature of the person issuing the authorization from the municipal office of the municipality with extended powers,

- f) the impression of the stamp of the municipal office of the municipality with extended powers.

(2) The template for the authorization of a person to ensure the safe crossing of children and school youth across a road is provided in Annex No. 16 to this decree.

p27.heading: Transitional provision
p27.text: Traffic signs and traffic devices installed before the effective date of this decree, which do not comply with this decree in their design, shall cease to be valid upon the expiration of December 31, 2025.

p29.text: This decree shall enter into force on January 1, 2016.

p28.heading: Repealing provisions
p28.text:
The following are hereby repealed:

- 1. Decree of the Ministry of Transport and Communications No. 30/2001 Sb., implementing the rules of road traffic and the regulation and direction of road traffic.

- 2. Decree No. 153/2003 Sb., amending Decree No. 30/2001 Sb., implementing the rules of road traffic and the regulation and direction of road traffic.

- 3. Decree No. 176/2004 Sb., amending Decree No. 30/2001 Sb., implementing the rules of road traffic and the regulation and direction of road traffic, as amended by Decree No. 153/2003 Sb.

- 4. Decree No. 193/2006 Sb., amending Decree of the Ministry of Transport and Communications No. 30/2001 Sb., implementing the rules of road traffic and the regulation and direction of road traffic, as amended by later regulations.

- 5. Decree No. 507/2006 Sb., amending Decree of the Ministry of Transport and Communications No. 30/2001 Sb., implementing the rules of road traffic and the regulation and direction of road traffic, as amended by later regulations.

- 6. Decree No. 202/2008 Sb., amending Decree of the Ministry of Transport and Communications No. 30/2001 Sb., implementing the rules of road traffic and the regulation and direction of road traffic, as amended by later regulations.

- 7. Decree No. 91/2009 Sb., amending Decree of the Ministry of Transport and Communications No. 30/2001 Sb., implementing the rules of road traffic and the regulation and direction of road traffic, as amended by later regulations.

- 8. Decree No. 247/2010 Sb., amending Decree No. 30/2001 Sb., implementing the rules of road traffic and the regulation and direction of road traffic, as amended by later regulations.

- 9. Decree No. 290/2011 Sb., amending Decree of the Ministry of Transport and Communications No. 30/2001 Sb., implementing the rules of road traffic and the regulation and direction of road traffic, as amended by later regulations.

**Minister:**

**Ing. Ťok v. r.**

sprilohac1kvyhlas.text:
Warning traffic signs

| Number | Illustration | Name, meaning and use |
| A 1a | | Curve to the right This sign warns of a curve (directional arc) to the right, the safe negotiation of which requires a significant reduction in driving speed. |
| A 1b | | Curve to the left This sign warns of a curve (directional arc) to the left, the safe negotiation of which requires a significant reduction in driving speed. |
| A 2a | | Double curve, first to the right This sign warns of two or more consecutive curves (directional arcs), the first of which is to the right, and the safe negotiation of which requires a significant reduction in driving speed. |
| A 2b | | Double curve, first to the left This sign warns of two or more consecutive curves (directional arcs), the first of which is to the left, and the safe negotiation of which requires a significant reduction in driving speed. |
| A 3 | | Intersection This sign warns of an intersection where the right of way is not regulated by vertical traffic signs. In urban areas, it is used exceptionally, e.g., to warn of an intersection where, unlike its preceding course, the road is no longer a main road and the provision on the right of way for vehicles approaching from the right applies. |
| A 4 | | Roundabout intersection This sign warns of a roundabout intersection in justified cases. |
| A 5a | | Dangerous descent This sign warns of a section of road where local conditions make the descent dangerous. The sign indicates the actual maximum longitudinal slope of the road. |
| A 5b | | Dangerous ascent This sign warns of a section of road with a steep ascent. The sign indicates the actual maximum longitudinal slope of the road. |
| A 6a | | Road narrows from both sides This sign warns of a place where the road significantly narrows from both sides compared to the preceding section. |
| A 6b | | Road narrows from one side This sign warns of a place where the road significantly narrows from one side compared to the preceding section. The symbol of the sign is designed to correspond to the actual narrowing of the road from either the left or the right side. |
| A 7a | | Uneven road This sign warns of bumps, potholes, holes and other unevenness in the road or a section with an uneven road surface, requiring increased caution or a significant reduction in driving speed. If there are ruts in the road, the sign is supplemented with an additional panel with the appropriate symbol or inscription. |
| A 7b | | Speed bump This sign warns of an artificial unevenness on the road, the purpose of which is mainly to reduce the speed of moving vehicles. |
| A 8 | | Danger of skidding This sign warns of a place or section of road where a vehicle may skid even under unhindered weather conditions. If the danger threatens under certain conditions, the validity of this sign is limited by an additional panel with the appropriate symbol. |
| A 9 | | Two-way traffic This sign warns of a section of road where, unlike the preceding section, vehicle traffic is temporarily or permanently conducted in both directions. The sign also marks the end of a directionally divided road. |
| A 10 | | Traffic lights This sign warns drivers of a place where traffic on the road is controlled by traffic lights that the driver would not otherwise expect, or that are not visible from a sufficient distance given local conditions. |
| A 11 | | Pedestrian crossing This sign warns of a pedestrian crossing that the driver would not otherwise expect and that is not visible from a sufficient distance. |
| A 12a | | Pedestrians This sign warns of a place or section of road with a possible increased presence of pedestrians or frequent crossing of pedestrians across the road in a place where there is no pedestrian crossing, e.g., at a crossing point. |
| A 12b | | Children This sign warns of a place or section of road in the vicinity of which children often move or gather, cross the road, or where there is an increased danger of them suddenly running into the road. |
| A 12c | | Persons on personal transporters This sign warns of a place or section of road in the vicinity of which persons on personal transporters often move or gather, cross the road, or where there is an increased danger of them suddenly entering the road. |
| A 13 | | Animals This sign warns of a place or section of road where domestic animals may frequently appear on the road. Another designated animal symbol may also be used on the sign. |
| A 14 | | Wild animals This sign warns of a place or section of road where wild animals may frequently appear on the road. Another designated symbol of a wild animal may also be used on the sign. |
| A 15 | | Road works This sign warns of work on the road or in its immediate vicinity that could endanger the safety of road traffic or where workers performing this activity could be endangered by traffic. |
| A 16 | | Side wind This sign warns of a place with the danger of sudden gusts of side wind that could endanger the safety of road traffic. If the sign is permanent, it is usually supplemented by a windsock, from whose movement the direction and strength of the wind are apparent. |
| A 17 | | Flying gravel This sign warns of a place or section of road where loose gravel is present on the road, which could endanger the safety of road users. |
| A 18 | | Rocks on road This sign warns of a place or section of road where rocks or soil may be present due to landslides or falling rocks onto the road. |
| A 19 | | Cyclists This sign warns of a place where cyclists enter or cross the road, or a section where cyclists are frequently present. |
| A 20 | | Aircraft This sign warns of a place where aircraft fly at a low altitude over the road. |
| A 21 | | Tunnel This sign warns of a tunnel where, in addition to general road traffic rules, special rules for tunnel traffic apply. |
| A 22 | | Other danger This sign warns of a danger other than that which can be indicated by the appropriate warning sign. The description or symbol of the other danger is usually indicated on a suitable additional panel. |
| A 23 | | Traffic jam This sign warns of the danger of a queue of stationary or slow-moving vehicles. |
| A 24 | | Black ice This sign warns of a section with an increased danger of black ice on the road. |
| A 25 | | Tram This sign warns of a place where a tram crosses the direction of travel of other vehicles. |
| A 26 | | Fog This sign warns of the danger of fog or smoke. |
| A 27 | | Accident This sign warns of the location of a traffic accident. |
| A 28 | | Dangerous shoulder This sign warns of a section of road where it is dangerous to drive onto the shoulder. |
| A 29 | | Railway crossing with barriers This sign warns of a railway crossing equipped with barriers. |
| A 30 | | Railway crossing without barriers This sign warns of a railway crossing not equipped with barriers. |
| A 31a | | Advance warning board (240 m) This sign is placed at a distance of 240 m before the railway crossing it warns of. The diagonal stripes point towards the center of the road. Above the sign, the sign “Railway crossing with barriers” or “Railway crossing without barriers” is placed; in case of their use to mark a crossing on a diverging road, an additional panel “Directional arrow” is inserted between these signs. |
| A 31b | | Advance warning board (160 m) This sign is placed at a distance of 160 m before the railway crossing it warns of. The diagonal stripes point towards the center of the road. In case of its use to mark a crossing on a diverging road, an additional panel “Directional arrow” is placed above this sign. |
| A 31c | | Advance warning board (80 m) This sign is placed at a distance of 80 m before the railway crossing it warns of. The diagonal stripe points towards the center of the road. In case of its use to mark a crossing on a diverging road, an additional panel “Directional arrow” is placed above this sign. |
| A 32a | | Warning cross for single-track railway crossing This sign warns of a single-track railway crossing. If the driver is obliged to stop the vehicle before the railway crossing, they shall stop before this sign. |
| A 32b | | Warning cross for multi-track railway crossing This sign warns of a multi-track railway crossing, where increased caution is required in case of simultaneous passage of trains from both directions. If the driver is obliged to stop the vehicle before the railway crossing, they shall stop before this sign. |
| A 33 | | Movable bridge This sign warns of a drawbridge or other movable bridge. |
| A 34 | | Embankment This sign warns of a section of road leading to an unprotected embankment or bank of a body of water. |
| --- | --- | --- |

sprilohac2kvyhlas.text:
Signs Regulating Priority

| Number | Illustration | Name, Meaning, and Use |
| P 1 | | Intersection with a minor road This sign warns of an intersection outside of a municipality and designates the major road. The sign may also depict only a left connection, a right connection, a left oblique connection, a right oblique connection, or a combination thereof. In the case of a more complex intersection or an intersection where the major road does not proceed in a straight direction, an appropriate supplementary panel indicating the shape of the intersection shall be used below this sign. |
| P 2 | | Major road This sign designates, especially within a municipality, the major road at the nearest intersection or within a large or more complex intersection. |
| P 3 | | End of major road This sign informs that at the nearest intersection, this road is no longer major. |
| P 4 | | Give Way! This sign designates a minor road. The sign may also be used within a larger or more complex intersection or at a place where the driver is instructed, reminded, or emphasized to give way. |
| P 5 | | Give Way to Tram! This sign is used at a place where the driver is obliged to give way to a tram, especially outside an intersection. |
| P 6 | | Stop, Give Way! This sign instructs the driver to stop the vehicle at a place from which there is a proper view into the intersection, and to give way. The sign may also be used within a larger or more complex intersection. The sign is also used before a railway crossing in combination with a warning cross in cases where it is necessary to instruct the driver to stop the vehicle. |
| P 7 | | Priority to oncoming vehicles This sign instructs the driver to give way to an oncoming vehicle in cases where oncoming vehicles cannot safely pass each other. |
| P 8 | | Priority over oncoming vehicles This sign informs the driver that the driver of an oncoming vehicle may enter the following section of the road only if their vehicle and the oncoming vehicle can safely pass each other. The sign is installed in the opposite direction of travel to the „Priority to oncoming vehicles“ sign. |
| --- | --- | --- |

sprilohac3kvyhlas.text:
Prohibitory Signs

| Number | Illustration | Name, Meaning and Use |
| B 1 | | No Entry for All Vehicles in Both Directions The sign prohibits the entry of all types of vehicles. |
| B 2 | | No Entry for All Vehicles The sign prohibits the entry of all types of vehicles into a one-way road where traffic flows in the opposite direction. |
| B 3 | | No Entry for All Motor Vehicles Except Motorcycles Without Sidecar The sign prohibits the entry of all motor vehicles except motorcycles without a sidecar. The prohibition does not apply to trams and non-motorized vehicles. |
| B 4 | | No Entry for Lorries The sign prohibits the entry of lorries and special purpose vehicles. The sign also prohibits the entry of tractors with a maximum authorized mass exceeding 3500 kg and a design speed of 80 km.h-1 or more. The sign does not apply to motorhomes. If a maximum authorized mass value is indicated on a supplementary plate or on the sign below the lorry symbol, the sign applies to vehicles with a maximum authorized mass exceeding this value. For a vehicle combination, the sum of the maximum authorized masses of all vehicles in the combination is decisive. |
| B 5 | | No Entry for Buses The sign prohibits the entry of buses. |
| B 6 | | No Entry for Tractors The sign prohibits the entry of tractors, including single-axle tractors, motorized handcarts, and self-propelled work machines. |
| B 7 | | No Entry for Motorcycles The sign prohibits the entry of motorcycles. |
| B 8 | | No Entry for Bicycles The sign prohibits cyclists from entering and riding a bicycle. Leading a bicycle by a cyclist is permitted. |
| B 9 | | No Entry for Animal-Drawn Vehicles The sign prohibits the entry of animal-drawn vehicles. |
| B 10 | | No Entry for Handcarts The sign prohibits the entry of handcarts with an immediate width greater than 600 mm. |
| B 11 | | No Entry for All Motor Vehicles The sign prohibits the entry of all motor vehicles. |
| B 12 | | No Entry for Indicated Vehicles The sign prohibits the entry of vehicles of the indicated types. |
| B 13 | | No Entry for Vehicles Whose Actual Mass Exceeds the Indicated Limit The sign prohibits the entry of vehicles whose actual mass exceeds the value indicated on the sign. In the case of a vehicle combination, this value applies to each vehicle in the combination separately. If the sign is supplemented by a supplementary plate with the inscription „Jediné vozidlo ... t“ (Single vehicle ... t), a vehicle whose actual mass, and in the case of a vehicle combination, the actual mass of all vehicles in the combination, exceeds the value on the sign but not the value on the supplementary plate, may enter the section thus marked. |
| B 14 | | No Entry for Vehicles Whose Actual Mass Per Axle Exceeds the Indicated Limit The sign prohibits the entry of vehicles whose actual mass per axle exceeds the value indicated on the sign. |
| B 15 | | No Entry for Vehicles Whose Width Exceeds the Indicated Limit The sign prohibits the entry of vehicles whose actual width, including load and rearview mirrors, is greater than the value indicated on the sign. |
| B 16 | | No Entry for Vehicles Whose Height Exceeds the Indicated Limit The sign prohibits the entry of vehicles whose actual height, including load, is greater than the value indicated on the sign. |
| B 17 | | No Entry for Vehicles or Combinations Whose Length Exceeds the Indicated Limit The sign prohibits the entry of vehicles or vehicle combinations whose actual length, including load, is greater than the value indicated on the sign. |
| B 18 | | No Entry for Vehicles Transporting Dangerous Goods The sign prohibits the entry of vehicles transporting explosives, highly flammable or otherwise dangerous goods and marked in accordance with another legal regulation3). |
| B 19 | | No Entry for Vehicles Transporting Goods That May Endanger the Environment The sign prohibits the entry of vehicles transporting crude oil or petroleum products or other substances that could endanger the environment, especially by polluting water. The quantity and, if applicable, the nature of the load may be indicated on a supplementary plate. |
| B 20a | | Maximum Permitted Speed The sign prohibits the driver from exceeding the speed indicated on the sign by a number in kilometers per hour. The validity of the sign is also terminated by the signs „Motorway“, „End of Motorway“, „Road for Motor Vehicles“, „End of Road for Motor Vehicles“, „Built-up Area“ and „End of Built-up Area“. |
| B 20b | | End of Maximum Permitted Speed The sign terminates the validity of the preceding „Maximum Permitted Speed“ sign. Note: After this sign, the maximum permitted speed resulting from general traffic regulations or established by the relevant zone traffic sign applies. If the validity of the preceding „Maximum Permitted Speed“ sign is limited by a supplementary plate, the relevant supplementary plate shall also be placed below the sign. |
| B 21a | | No Overtaking The sign prohibits the driver from overtaking a motor vehicle on the left, with the exception of motorcycles without a sidecar. If the sign is placed above the roadway, it applies to the entire width of the roadway in the given direction of travel. |
| B 21b | | End of No Overtaking The sign terminates the validity of the „No Overtaking“ sign. |
| B 22a | | No Overtaking for Lorries The sign prohibits the driver of a lorry with a maximum authorized mass exceeding 3500 kg or a tractor with a maximum authorized mass exceeding 3500 kg and a design speed of 80 km.h-1 or more, from overtaking a motor vehicle on the left. On a roadway with two or more lanes in one direction of travel, the driver of a lorry with a maximum authorized mass exceeding 3500 kg or a tractor with a maximum authorized mass exceeding 3500 kg and a design speed of 80 km.h-1 or more, must not use any lane other than the right-hand lane, unless it is necessary for bypassing. If the sign is placed above the roadway, it applies to the entire width of the roadway in the given direction of travel. |
| B 22b | | End of No Overtaking for Lorries The sign terminates the validity of the „No Overtaking for Lorries“ sign. |
| B 23a | | No Sound Warning Signals The sign prohibits the use of sound warning signals. |
| B 23b | | End of No Sound Warning Signals The sign terminates the validity of the „No Sound Warning Signals“ sign. |
| B 24a | | No Right Turn The sign prohibits turning right at the nearest intersection, onto a service road at its junction, or into a place located outside the road. |
| B 24b | | No Left Turn The sign prohibits turning left at the nearest intersection, onto a service road at its junction, or into a place located outside the road. |
| B 25 | | No U-Turn The sign prohibits making a U-turn. |
| B 26 | | End of All Prohibitions The sign terminates the validity of all prohibitions established for moving vehicles. |
| B 27 | | Stop The sign prohibits the driver from continuing to drive without stopping the vehicle in the area near the sign. The purpose of the prohibition can be specified in the lower part of the sign, for example, with the inscription „POLICE“, „CONTROL“, etc. |
| B 28 | | No Stopping The sign prohibits stopping or standing, i.e., bringing the vehicle to a standstill, except in cases caused by circumstances in road traffic, such as a stationary queue of vehicles, etc. If a time is indicated in the red circle in the lower part of the sign, the prohibition applies only during the specified time. |
| B 29 | | No Standing The sign prohibits standing, i.e., bringing the vehicle to a standstill for a period longer than the time strictly necessary for the immediate boarding or alighting of transported persons or for the immediate loading and unloading of goods. If a time is indicated in the red circle in the lower part of the sign, the prohibition applies only during the specified time. |
| B 30 | | No Pedestrian Entry The sign prohibits pedestrians from entering the road located in the area beyond the sign. |
| B 30a | | No Entry for Personal Transporters The sign prohibits persons on personal transporters from entering and riding a personal transporter. Leading a personal transporter by a pedestrian is permitted. |
| B 31 | | No Entry for Riders on Animals The sign prohibits a rider on an animal from entering the road beyond the sign with the animal; the prohibition also applies to leading or driving such an animal. |
| B 32 | | Other Prohibition The sign establishes a prohibition other than one that can be expressed by another prohibitory sign listed in this annex. The prohibition is expressed by a suitable inscription or a designated symbol placed inside the red circle. If the sign bears the inscription „PRŮJEZD ZAKÁZÁN“ (THROUGH TRAFFIC PROHIBITED), the driver must not enter the section thus marked without reason. Bypassing a complex traffic situation or shortening a route is not considered such a reason. If the sign bears the inscription „CNG“, „LPG“ or another inscription indicating the type of vehicle propulsion, vehicles with this type of propulsion are prohibited from entering the section thus marked. If the sign bears the inscription „SMOG“, driving (not just entry) is prohibited for designated vehicles in an area for which a smog situation has been declared according to a special legal regulation. |
| B 33 | | No Entry for Motor Vehicles with Trailers The sign prohibits the entry of vehicles with a trailer or semi-trailer. |
| B 34 | | Minimum Distance Between Vehicles The sign prohibits the driver of a vehicle from maintaining a distance from the vehicle in front of it that is less than the value indicated on the sign. |
| --- | --- | --- |

sprilohac4kvyhlas.text:
Mandatory Signs

| Number | Illustration | Name, meaning, and use |
| C 1 | | Roundabout This sign mandates driving through the intersection with a roundabout in the direction of the arrows shown on the sign. If the sign is supplemented by the sign „Give Way!“ or „Stop, Give Way!“, the driver entering the roundabout must give way to vehicles already on the roundabout. |
| C 2a | | Mandatory direction straight ahead This sign mandates driving straight ahead. |
| C 2b | | Mandatory direction right This sign mandates driving to the right. |
| C 2c | | Mandatory direction left This sign mandates driving to the left. |
| C 2d | | Mandatory direction straight ahead and right This sign mandates driving straight ahead and to the right. |
| C 2e | | Mandatory direction straight ahead and left This sign mandates driving straight ahead and to the left. |
| C 2f | | Mandatory direction right and left This sign mandates driving to the right and to the left. |
| C 3a | | Mandatory direction here right This sign mandates driving to the right immediately before the sign. |
| C 3b | | Mandatory direction here left This sign mandates driving to the left immediately before the sign. |
| C 4a | | Mandatory direction of passing right This sign mandates passing an island, obstacle, etc., to the right. |
| C 4b | | Mandatory direction of passing left This sign mandates passing an island, obstacle, etc., to the left. |
| C 4c | | Mandatory direction of passing right and left This sign mandates passing an island, obstacle, etc., to the right or to the left. |
| C 5a | | Snow chains This sign mandates the driver of a motor vehicle with three or more wheels to continue driving only after fitting snow (anti-skid) chains on at least two driving wheels. This obligation does not apply to vehicles equipped with studded tires. |
| C 5b | | Snow chains – end This sign terminates the validity of the mandatory sign „Snow chains“. |
| C 6a | | Minimum speed This sign mandates the driver, if other circumstances of road traffic permit, to drive at a speed of at least the number indicated on the sign in kilometers per hour. Circumstances for not adhering to the minimum permitted speed include, for example, high traffic density, poor road condition, fog, black ice, etc. |
| C 6b | | End of minimum speed This sign terminates the validity of the sign „Minimum speed“. |
| C 7a | | Pedestrian path This sign designates a pedestrian path. |
| C 7b | | End of pedestrian path This sign terminates the validity of the sign „Pedestrian path“. |
| C 8a | | Cycle path This sign designates a cycle path. |
| C 8b | | End of cycle path This sign terminates the validity of the sign „Cycle path“. |
| C 9a | | Shared pedestrian and cycle path This sign designates a path for pedestrians and cyclists where the lane for pedestrians and the lane for cyclists are not separated. |
| C 9b | | End of shared pedestrian and cycle path This sign terminates the validity of the sign „Shared pedestrian and cycle path“. |
| C 10a | | Divided pedestrian and cycle path This sign designates a path for pedestrians and cyclists where the lane for pedestrians and the lane for cyclists are separated. The placement of symbols expresses the actual arrangement of lanes for pedestrians and cyclists. |
| C 10b | | End of divided pedestrian and cycle path This sign terminates the validity of the sign „Divided pedestrian and cycle path“. |
| C 11a | | Bridleway This sign designates a bridleway. |
| C 11b | | End of bridleway This sign terminates the validity of the sign „Bridleway“. |
| C 12a | | Mandatory lane This sign mandates the driver of the indicated type of vehicle to use the lane so designated in the given direction. Drivers of other types of vehicles may enter such a designated lane only when overtaking, turning, making a U-turn, or entering or exiting a road, provided that no driver of a vehicle for which the lane is designated is endangered or restricted. In the case of a mandatory lane for another type of vehicle or for a specific purpose, the appropriate designated symbol or suitable inscription shall be marked in the blue field. |
| C 12b | | End of mandatory lane This sign terminates the validity of the sign „Mandatory lane“. |
| C 13a | | Switch on lights This sign mandates the driver to switch on the lights prescribed for a vehicle driving in reduced visibility. |
| C 13b | | Switch on lights – end This sign terminates the validity of the sign „Switch on lights“. This does not affect the obligation to use the prescribed vehicle lighting arising from the general regulation of road traffic. |
| C 14a | | Other command This sign imposes a command other than one that can be imposed by another mandatory sign. The command is expressed by a suitable inscription on the sign, for example, „Cyclist, dismount“, „Cross to the other side“, etc. |
| C 14b | | End of other command This sign terminates the validity of the respective sign „Other command“. |
| C 15a | | Winter equipment This sign mandates the driver of a motor vehicle of category M or N to continue driving only if the conditions stipulated by law for vehicle operation in winter are met, regardless of the date and current or anticipated weather conditions. An additional table may be placed below the sign indicating a different regulation of the period of validity. |
| C 15b | | Winter equipment – end This sign terminates the validity of the sign „Winter equipment“. |
| --- | --- | --- |

sprilohac5kvyhlas.text:
Informative Signs

- 1. Informative Zone Signs

| Number | Illustration | Name, Meaning and Use |
| IZ 1a | | Motorway This sign designates a road on which, in addition to general road traffic rules, special rules for motorway traffic apply. The use of a road marked in this way is subject to a toll or time-based fee according to a special legal regulation4), unless otherwise indicated by an additional supplementary plate. On a road for motor vehicles, the sign also indicates the end of the road for motor vehicles. |
| IZ 1b | | End of Motorway This sign indicates the end of a road where special rules for motorway traffic apply. |
| IZ 2a | | Road for Motor Vehicles This sign designates a road on which, in addition to general road traffic rules, special rules for road for motor vehicle traffic apply. The use of a road marked in this way is not subject to a fee, unless otherwise indicated by an additional supplementary plate placed below this sign. On a motorway, the sign also indicates the end of the motorway. |
| IZ 2b | | End of Road for Motor Vehicles This sign indicates the end of a road where special rules for road for motor vehicle traffic apply. |
| IZ 3a | | Tunnel This sign indicates the entrance to a tunnel, where, in addition to general road traffic rules, special rules for tunnel traffic apply. For tunnels longer than 500 m, their actual length shall be indicated in the lower part of the sign or on a supplementary plate. For tunnels longer than 3,000 m, the remaining length of the tunnel shall be indicated every 1000 m. |
| IZ 3b | | End of Tunnel This sign indicates the point where the special rules for tunnel traffic cease to apply. |
| IZ 4a | | Municipality This sign indicates the beginning of an area where rules for traffic in a municipality apply and informs about the name of the municipality. |
| IZ 4b | | End of Municipality This sign indicates the end of a municipality. |
| IZ 4c | | Municipality in the Language of a National Minority This sign has the same meaning as the „Municipality“ sign, is placed together with it, and informs about the name of the municipality in the language of a national minority. |
| IZ 4d | | End of Municipality in the Language of a National Minority This sign indicates the end of a municipality in the language of a national minority. |
| IZ 5a | | Residential Zone This sign indicates an area where, in addition to general road traffic rules, special rules for traffic in a residential zone apply. |
| IZ 5b | | End of Residential Zone This sign indicates the end of a residential zone. The sign may also be placed only on the left side of the roadway when exiting a residential zone, from the opposite side of the „Residential Zone“ sign. |
| IZ 6a | | Pedestrian Zone This sign indicates an area, especially a part of a municipality, where, in addition to general road traffic rules, special rules for traffic in a pedestrian zone apply. The lower part of the sign shall indicate, with an appropriate inscription or symbol, which vehicles are permitted to enter this area and, if applicable, during what time. |
| IZ 6b | | End of Pedestrian Zone This sign indicates the end of a pedestrian zone. The sign may also be placed only on the left side of the roadway when exiting a residential zone, from the opposite side of the „Pedestrian Zone“ sign. |
| IZ 7a | | Low-Emission Zone This sign indicates an area in which a low-emission zone is established according to a special legal regulation. In this area, the operation of road motor vehicles is restricted to reduce air pollution from traffic. The lower part of the sign shall include a reference to the low-emission zone information system according to a special legal regulation. |
| IZ 7b | | End of Low-Emission Zone This sign indicates the end of a low-emission zone. The lower part of the sign shall include a reference to the low-emission zone information system according to a special legal regulation. The sign may also be placed only on the left side of the roadway when exiting a low-emission zone, from the opposite side of the „Low-Emission Zone“ sign. |
| IZ 8a | | Zone with Traffic Restriction This sign indicates an area, especially a part of a municipality, where a warning, prohibition, restriction, or command resulting from the respective illustration of the sign or signs applies, unless local road traffic regulation within the area stipulates otherwise. Traffic restrictions concerning only certain vehicles, times, etc., shall be indicated in the lower part of the signs with an appropriate inscription or symbol. The sign may be in a square format. If a mandatory or prohibitory traffic sign is depicted on the sign, this zone traffic sign has the character of a mandatory or prohibitory traffic sign. |
| IZ 8b | | End of Zone with Traffic Restriction This sign indicates the end of an area where a warning, prohibition, restriction, or command resulting from the respective „Zone with Traffic Restriction“ sign applies. |
| IZ 9a | | Cyclist Zone This sign indicates an area where, in addition to general road traffic rules, special rules for traffic in a cyclist zone apply. The lower part of the sign shall indicate, with an inscription or appropriate symbol, vehicles other than bicycles that are permitted to enter the cyclist zone. |
| IZ 9b | | End of Cyclist Zone This sign indicates the end of a cyclist zone. The sign may also be placed only on the left side of the roadway when exiting a cyclist zone, from the opposite side of the „Cyclist Zone“ sign. |
| IZ 10a | | Shared Zone This sign indicates an area where, in addition to general road traffic rules, special rules for traffic in a shared zone apply. |
| IZ 10b | | End of Shared Zone This sign indicates the end of a shared zone. The sign may also be placed only on the left side of the roadway when exiting a shared zone, from the opposite side of the „Shared Zone“ sign. |
| --- | --- | --- |

- 2. Informative Operational Signs

| Number | Illustration | Name, Meaning and Use |
| IP 1 | | Ring Road This sign indicates a ring road established for bypassing a municipality or its part. The name of the ring road or its sequential number, starting from the center of the municipality, may be indicated on the sign or its supplementary plate. The color scheme of the sign may differ from the illustration. On motorways and roads for motor vehicles, the ring road may be indicated by a symbol and the name or number of the ring road placed on an extension above the relevant part of a large-format directional informative sign. A change in the direction of the ring road is indicated by a combination of this sign and an appropriate supplementary plate in the form of a directional arrow. |
| IP 2 | | Speed Bump This sign immediately indicates an artificial unevenness on the roadway, the purpose of which is to reduce the speed of moving vehicles. |
| IP 3 | | Underpass or Overpass This sign informs about the proximity of an underpass or overpass. |
| IP 4a | | One-Way Traffic This sign indicates the direction of vehicle travel on a one-way road. |
| IP 4b | | One-Way Traffic This sign indicates the direction of vehicle travel on a one-way road. |
| IP 4c | | One-Way Traffic with Permitted Cyclist Traffic in Opposite Direction This sign indicates the direction of vehicle travel on a one-way road and indicates permission for cyclists to enter in the opposite direction. |
| IP 5 | | Recommended Speed This sign recommends to the driver, if other road traffic circumstances permit, a driving speed expressed on the sign by a number in kilometers per hour, which can be considered safe. Circumstances that must be taken into account include, for example, high traffic density, poor road condition, fog, black ice, etc. |
| IP 6 | | Pedestrian Crossing This sign indicates a pedestrian crossing marked by the horizontal sign „Pedestrian Crossing“, especially outside an intersection. |
| IP 7 | | Cyclist Crossing This sign indicates a cyclist crossing marked by the horizontal sign „Cyclist Crossing“, especially where a driver would not otherwise expect it. |
| IP 9 | | Emergency Stopping This sign indicates a place (area) reserved for emergency stopping of a vehicle. If the place is equipped with an emergency call box, the sign is supplemented with the inscription „SOS“ placed in the illustration of the bay. |
| IP 10a | | Dead End Road This sign indicates a road that ends further on or along which further travel is not possible. The sign may also be used to indicate a road along which further travel is not possible, with the exception of cyclists. |
| IP 10b | | Advance Warning of Dead End Road This sign indicates an advance warning of a road that ends further on or along which further travel is not possible. The sign may also be used as an advance warning of a road along which further travel is not possible, with the exception of cyclists. |
| IP 11a | | Parking This sign indicates a place where stopping and parking are permitted. The method of parking or the type of vehicle for which the parking lot is intended may be indicated on the sign or a supplementary plate. |
| IP 11b | | Parking Perpendicular or Angled Parking This sign indicates a place where stopping and parking are permitted. The symbol shown in the lower part of the sign indicates the prescribed method of parking. The position of the vehicle for perpendicular or angled parking must correspond to the position expressed by the symbol shown on the sign or supplementary plate. |
| IP 11c | | Parking Parallel Parking This sign indicates a place where stopping and parking are permitted. The symbol shown in the lower part of the sign indicates the prescribed method of parking. |
| IP 11d | | Parking on Pavement Perpendicular or Angled Parking This sign indicates a place where stopping and parking are permitted. The symbol shown in the lower part of the sign indicates the prescribed method of parking. The position of the vehicle for perpendicular or angled parking must correspond to the position expressed by the symbol shown on the sign or supplementary plate. A vehicle with a maximum permissible weight exceeding 3,500 kg, a single-axle tractor, a motorized hand truck, and a self-propelled work machine must not stop or park on the pavement. |
| IP 11e | | Parking on Pavement Parallel Parking This sign indicates a place where stopping and parking are permitted. The symbol shown in the lower part of the sign indicates the prescribed method of parking. A vehicle with a maximum permissible weight exceeding 3,500 kg, a single-axle tractor, a motorized hand truck, and a self-propelled work machine must not stop or park on the pavement. |
| IP 11f | | Parking Partial Parking on Pavement Perpendicular or Angled This sign indicates a place where stopping and parking are permitted. The symbol shown in the lower part of the sign indicates the prescribed method of parking. The position of the vehicle for perpendicular or angled parking must correspond to the position expressed by the symbol shown on the sign or supplementary plate. A vehicle with a maximum permissible weight exceeding 3,500 kg, a single-axle tractor, a motorized hand truck, and a self-propelled work machine must not stop or park on the pavement. |
| IP 11g | | Parking Partial Parking on Pavement Parallel This sign indicates a place where stopping and parking are permitted. The symbol shown in the lower part of the sign indicates the prescribed method of parking. The symbol may be reversed. A vehicle with a maximum permissible weight exceeding 3,500 kg, a single-axle tractor, a motorized hand truck, and a self-propelled work machine must not stop or park on the pavement. |
| IP 12 | | Reserved Parking This sign indicates a place where stopping and parking are permitted only for vehicles for which the parking lot is reserved. Information about who the parking lot is reserved for and, if applicable, during what time, is indicated on a supplementary plate or may be indicated on the sign instead of text. Outside the time for which the parking lot is reserved, drivers of other vehicles may also stop and park in such a marked place, unless otherwise stipulated by general or local road traffic regulations. Any prescribed method of parking is indicated similarly to the „Parking“ signs indicating the method of parking. A sign on which a symbol of a facility or space for persons in wheelchairs is displayed is always supplemented by the horizontal traffic sign „Reserved Parking for a Vehicle Transporting a Severely Disabled Person or a Person with Severe Mobility Impairment“. A sign with an electric vehicle symbol supplemented by a supplementary plate with the text „CHARGING ONLY“ indicates a place reserved for vehicle parking only for the duration of charging. This sign is always supplemented by the horizontal traffic sign „Inscription on Roadway“ with a charging station symbol. |
| IP 13a | | Covered Parking This sign informs about a covered parking lot or parking garage. Any prescribed method of parking is indicated similarly to the „Parking“ signs indicating the method of parking. |
| IP 13b | | Parking with Parking Disc This sign indicates a parking lot where the driver must, at the beginning of parking, visibly place a disc in the vehicle and set the start time of parking on it, which must not be changed until departure. Any prescribed method of parking is indicated similarly to the „Parking“ signs indicating the method of parking. |
| IP 13c | | Parking with Parking Meter This sign indicates a paid parking lot. The driver must follow the information on the sign, supplementary plate, or parking meter (clock). Any prescribed method of parking is indicated similarly to the „Parking“ signs indicating the method of parking. |
| IP 13d | | P+R Parking This sign indicates a parking lot from which one can continue travel by public transport. |
| IP 13e | | K+R Parking This sign indicates a parking lot where one can stop for the purpose of dropping off and picking up passengers. |
| IP 13f | | P+D Parking This sign indicates a parking lot intended for parking vehicles whose driver immediately continues their journey as a passenger in another vehicle. |
| IP 16 | | Lane Arrangement This sign indicates the number and arrangement of traffic lanes. Lanes may also be indicated on the sign in the opposite direction. |
| IP 17 | | Lane Arrangement This sign indicates the number and arrangement of traffic lanes. The sign also indicates which lane is continuous. |
| IP 18a | | Increase in Number of Lanes This sign indicates the number and arrangement of traffic lanes. |
| IP 18b | | Decrease in Number of Lanes This sign indicates the number and arrangement of traffic lanes. The sign also indicates which lane is continuous. |
| IP 18c | | Lane for Slow Vehicles This sign indicates the number and arrangement of traffic lanes. The sign also indicates an auxiliary lane on a motorway and road for motor vehicles intended for motor vehicles that do not reach a speed of at least 80 km.h-1 in the thus marked section of the motorway or road for motor vehicles. Other motor vehicles use the lane for slow vehicles if their driving speed in the given section of the motorway or road for motor vehicles is significantly lower than the driving speed of other vehicles traveling in the same direction. |
| IP 19 | | Lane Discipline This sign indicates the method of lane discipline before an intersection or turning point and the prescribed direction of travel. In justified cases, the symbol of the relevant informative sign may also be used on the sign, provided that the relevant lane is intended for access only to that destination. By using the symbol of the supplementary plate „Permitted Direction for Cyclists“, a different method of lane discipline for cyclists in traffic lanes can be stipulated. |
| IP 20a | | Reserved Lane This sign indicates a reserved lane for public transport vehicles and its position in relation to other traffic lanes. The reserved lane is simultaneously marked on the road by horizontal signs, especially „Continuous Longitudinal Line“ or „Broken Longitudinal Line“ and the inscription „BUS“. The period for which the lane is reserved may be indicated in the lower part of the sign or on a supplementary plate. Lanes in the opposite direction may also be indicated on the sign. In the case of a reserved lane for another type of vehicle or for a specific purpose, the appropriate symbol or suitable inscription shall be indicated in the blue field. If the inscription TAXI is included in the blue field, the reserved lane is intended for taxi vehicles marked with a roof light with the inscription TAXI. The placement of a reserved lane for a certain type of vehicle in relation to other traffic lanes can be indicated by inserting the symbol of this traffic sign into another traffic sign indicating the number and arrangement of traffic lanes or the method of lane discipline. |
| IP 20b | | End of Reserved Lane This sign terminates a reserved lane. |
| IP 21 | | Lane Restriction This sign indicates the number and arrangement of traffic lanes and expresses a restriction or danger for a specific traffic lane resulting from the used symbol of the relevant vertical traffic sign. |
| IP 22 | | Change in Traffic Organization This sign, with an appropriate inscription and symbol, warns of a change in local road traffic regulation, a change in traffic organization, unusual road construction, a place with frequent accidents, etc. |
| IP 23a | | Overtaking Tram This sign indicates a place where a tram stopped at a stop can be overtaken on the left. |
| IP 23b | | Overtaking Tram (Driving Alongside Tram on the Left) This sign indicates a section of road where driving alongside a tram on the left is permitted. |
| IP 23c | | Public Transport Vehicles Exiting Tram Lane This sign indicates the exit point, especially for public transport buses, from a tram lane. If a driver of a vehicle for which a reserved lane is designated moves from the reserved lane into an adjacent lane, the driver of the vehicle traveling in that lane must allow them to do so by reducing speed or even stopping the vehicle. |
| IP 24 | | Escape Lane This sign informs about an escape lane where, in an emergency, for example, due to reduced braking effectiveness, a vehicle can be stopped by auxiliary means, such as an uphill slope, coarse gravel surface, etc. |
| IP 28a | | Toll Road This sign informs drivers entering the Czech Republic about the obligation to pay a time-based fee or toll. The text on the sign is usually provided in the language of the neighboring state and in English. |
| IP 28b | | Maximum Permitted Speeds This sign informs drivers entering the Czech Republic about generally prescribed maximum driving speeds in municipalities, outside municipalities, on motorways and roads for motor vehicles, and about the obligation of daytime running lights. |
| IP 29 | | Alternating Merge This sign indicates or emphasizes a place where, in parallel driving, alternating merging into the continuous lane applies. The symbol on the sign may be depicted in reverse. |
| IP 30 | | State Border This sign indicates the proximity of the state border when entering the Czech Republic. When exiting the Czech Republic, the name of the neighboring state is indicated on the sign. |
| IP 31a | | Speed Measurement This sign indicates the beginning of a section where speed is measured by municipal police. |
| IP 31b | | End of Speed Measurement This sign indicates the end of a section where speed is measured by municipal police. |
| IP 32 | | Safe Distance This sign, in conjunction with the relevant horizontal traffic sign „Safe Distance“, informs about a safe distance between moving vehicles. Two arrows between two consecutive vehicles indicate the minimum safe distance between vehicles on the given road at the maximum permitted speed under normal conditions. |
| --- | --- | --- |

- 3. Informative Directional Signs

| Number | Illustration | Name, Meaning and Use |
| IS 1a IS 1b IS 1c | | Directional Sign for Access to Motorway (Straight, Left or Right) These signs inform about the direction to the indicated destination(s) using a motorway. The motorway symbol can also be used alone on the sign. |
| IS 1d IS 1e IS 1f | | Directional Sign Before Motorway Entrance (Straight, Left or Right) These signs indicate the direction of entry to the motorway, its destination(s), and number. The route beyond the traffic sign leads directly onto the motorway itself. |
| IS 2a IS 2b IS 2c | | Directional Sign for Access to Road for Motor Vehicles (Straight, Left or Right) This sign informs about the direction to the indicated destination(s) using a road for motor vehicles. |
| IS 2d IS 2e IS 2f | | Directional Sign Before Entrance to Road for Motor Vehicles (Straight, Left or Right) This sign indicates the direction of entry to the road for motor vehicles, its destination(s), and number. |
| IS 3a IS 3b IS 3c | | Directional Sign with Destination (Straight, Left or Right) This sign informs about the direction and usually the distance in kilometers to the indicated destination(s). The road number is indicated in a frame. |
| IS 4a IS 4b IS 4c | | Directional Sign with Local Destination (Straight, Left or Right) This sign informs about the direction and usually the distance in kilometers to the indicated local destination(s). |
| IS 5 | | Directional Sign with Other Destination This sign informs, with an appropriate inscription or symbol, about the direction and, if applicable, the distance to a destination other than a municipality or its part. An „other destination“ means, in particular, a publicly accessible object that is not considered a local destination and for which no other traffic sign is designated. Other destinations include, in particular, airports, railway stations, exhibition grounds, industrial zones, shopping zones, etc. |
| IS 6a | | Intersection Marking This sign usually informs on motorways and roads for motor vehicles about an approaching grade-separated intersection with another road and its number. In the case of two motorways crossing, the motorway junction symbol is used on the sign. |
| IS 6b | | Advance Direction Sign for Intersection This sign informs about the destination(s) in the straight direction and in the turning direction. When placed above the roadway, the arrows simultaneously indicate the number and arrangement of traffic lanes. Instead of the exit symbol, a junction symbol may be used on the sign, or the extension with the intersection marking may not be placed at all. When the sign is used at an intersection, the distance is not indicated. |
| IS 6d | | Advance Direction Sign for Intersection This sign informs about the destination(s) and the number and arrangement of traffic lanes in the straight direction and in the turning direction. The sign cannot be placed above the roadway. The sign can also be used as an advance warning sign, in which case the appropriate distance is indicated on the sign. |
| IS 6e | | Directional Sign for Straight Ahead This sign informs about the destination(s) and the number of traffic lanes in the straight direction. When placed above the roadway, the arrows simultaneously indicate the method of lane discipline before the intersection. The sign can also be used as an advance warning sign, in which case the appropriate distance is indicated on the sign. |
| IS 6f | | Directional Sign Before Turn This sign informs, before the turning point, about the destination(s) and the number of traffic lanes in the turning direction. When placed above the roadway, the arrows simultaneously indicate the method of lane discipline before the intersection. The sign can also be used as an advance warning sign, in which case the appropriate distance is indicated on the sign. |
| IS 6g | | Directional Sign Before Turn This sign usually informs, at the beginning of the turning lane, about the destination(s) and the number of traffic lanes in the turning direction. When placed above the roadway, the arrows simultaneously indicate the method of lane discipline before the intersection. The sign can also be used as an advance warning sign, in which case the appropriate distance is indicated on the sign. |
| IS 7a | | Directional Sign for Turn This sign informs, at the turning point, about the destination(s) on the connecting road. |
| IS 7b | | Directional Sign for Exit This sign informs about the exit number from a motorway or road for motor vehicles to another road and indicates the turning point. |
| IS 8a | | Long-Distance Direction Sign with Arrow This sign informs about destinations and the direction to the indicated destinations and serves as confirmation of the correct choice of further direction of travel. |
| IS 8b | | Long-Distance Direction Sign with Distances This sign informs about destinations and the distance in kilometers to the indicated destinations and serves as confirmation of the correct choice of further direction of travel. |
| IS 9a | | Advance Direction Sign for At-Grade Intersection This sign informs about the direction to the indicated destinations from the intersection and indicates the routing of the main and secondary roads. The distance to this intersection may be indicated in the lower part of the sign. |
| IS 9b | | Advance Direction Sign for Roundabout This sign informs about the direction from the roundabout to the indicated destinations. |
| IS 9c | | Advance Direction Sign for Intersection with Restriction This sign informs about a traffic restriction or danger beyond the nearest intersection. |
| IS 9d | | Advance Direction Sign for Intersection with Restriction This sign informs about a traffic restriction or danger beyond the nearest intersection and about the direction of the detour. |
| IS 9e | | Advance Direction Sign for Intersection with Restriction This sign informs, before an intersection, that it is prohibited to proceed in the indicated direction at the next intersection, and in this case, informs about a possible detour direction. |
| IS 10a | | Advance Direction Sign for Change of Direction This sign informs, in particular, about a change in the direction of travel on a road or about the method of passing a place on a road, or about the distance to such a place, and indicates the number and arrangement of traffic lanes. |
| IS 10b | | Advance Direction Sign for Change of Direction This sign informs, in particular, about a change in the direction of travel on a road or about the method of passing a place on a road, or about the distance to such a place, and indicates the number and arrangement of traffic lanes. |
| IS 10c | | Advance Direction Sign for Change of Direction Before Obstacle This sign informs, in particular, about a change in the direction of travel before a permanent obstacle. |
| IS 10d | | Advance Direction Sign for Change of Direction with Restriction This sign informs, in particular, about a change in the direction of travel on a road or about the method of passing a place on a road, or about the distance to such a place, and indicates the number and arrangement of traffic lanes, and simultaneously indicates a restriction applicable to the respective traffic lane. |
| IS 11a | | Advance Direction Sign for Detour This sign informs about a road closure or restriction. The sign shall indicate the symbol of the relevant prohibitory sign according to the nature of the closure; the length of the detour and, if applicable, other supplementary information may also be indicated. |
| IS 11b | | Directional Sign for Detour This sign indicates the direction and course of a detour. The sign can be used to indicate an alternative route for a specific type of vehicle indicated on the sign by a designated symbol or inscription in the direction of travel to a specific destination. |
| IS 11c | | Directional Sign for Detour This sign indicates the direction and course of a detour or alternative route. |
| IS 11d | | Directional Sign for Detour This sign indicates the direction and course of a detour or alternative route. The sign shall indicate the number of the motorway or road for which the detour is indicated. In addition to the number, the type of vehicle for which the sign applies may also be indicated on the sign. |
| IS 12a | | Directional Sign for Alternative Motorway Route (Straight, Left or Right) These signs inform about the number of the alternative route and indicate the direction of its continuation. |
| IS 12b | | |
| IS 12c | | |
| IS 12d | | Alternative Route This sign usually informs on a motorway about the beginning of a permanently marked alternative motorway route and its number. If it is expedient in such a case, the symbol and the relevant motorway destination may also be indicated on the sign. If a directional arrow is indicated on the sign, it can also be used to indicate the course of the alternative route at the next intersection. |
| IS 13 | | Nearby Destination Sign This sign informs about the nearest destination and the distance in kilometers to it. The sign is usually placed at the exit from a municipality. |
| IS 14 | | Boundary of Territorial Unit This sign indicates the boundary of a territorial unit and informs about its name. |
| IS 15a | | Other Name This sign informs, for example, about the name of a river, mountain range, nature reserve, part of a municipality, etc. The sign may be supplemented by an appropriate symbol. |
| IS 15b | | Other Name This sign informs about a structural object on the road (e.g., tunnel or bridge) and its length. |
| IS 16a | | Motorway Number This sign informs about the motorway number. |
| IS 16b | | Road Number This sign informs about the class and number of the road. Numbers 1 to 99 indicate a first-class road; a three-digit number indicates a second-class road; the designation of third-class roads is usually not used on the sign. To indicate the direction to a specific road, the sign is used with a dashed frame. |
| IS 17 | | International Road Number This sign informs about the international road number. |
| IS 18a | | Kilometre Marker This sign indicates the chainage (distance) from the beginning of the road. The sign may be supplemented by the designation of the side of a directionally divided road. |
| IS 18b | | Kilometre Marker This sign indicates the chainage (distance) from the beginning of the road. An arrow on the sign indicates the direction to the nearest emergency call box. The sign may be supplemented by the designation of the side of a directionally divided road. |
