| IS 19a IS 19b IS 19c | | Directional Sign for Cyclists (Straight, Left or Right) This sign informs about the direction and distance in kilometers to the indicated destination(s) of a cycling route and about the number (or other designation) of this route. |
| IS 20 | | Advance Direction Sign for Cyclists This sign informs, for example, about the direction and number of a cycling route, its course at a crossing or intersection, etc. |
| IS 21a IS 21b IS 21c | | Directional Plate for Cyclists (Straight, Left or Right) This sign informs about the number (or other designation) of a cycling route and indicates its course. |
| IS 21d | | End of Cycling Route This sign informs about the end of a marked cycling route. |
| IS 22a IS 22b IS 22c IS 22d IS 22e IS 22f | | Street Name or Other Public Space Designation This sign informs about the name and course and, if applicable, the direction to the indicated street or other public space. |
| IS 23 | | Advance Direction Sign for Cultural or Tourist Destination This sign informs about a nearby cultural or tourist destination. The symbol on the sign schematically depicts the respective destination. |
| IS 24a | | Cultural or Tourist Destination This sign informs about the direction to the indicated tourist or cultural destination. The sign may be supplemented by a designated symbol. |
| IS 24b | | Directional Sign for Cultural or Tourist Destination This sign informs about the direction and distance to the indicated tourist or cultural destination. The sign may be supplemented by a designated symbol. |
| IS 24c | | Municipal Destination This sign informs about the direction to the indicated municipal destination. The sign may be supplemented by a designated or other appropriate symbol and may also be in a different color scheme that is not interchangeable with another traffic sign. |
| --- | --- | --- |

- 4. Other Informative Signs

| Number | Illustration | Name, Meaning and Use |
| IJ 1 | | Police This sign indicates the nearest Police of the Czech Republic station, or the direction or distance to it. |
| IJ 2 | | Hospital This sign indicates the nearest hospital, or the direction or distance to it. |
| IJ 3 | | First Aid This sign indicates the nearest first aid medical service point, or the direction or distance to it. |
| IJ 4a | | Stop Sign This sign indicates a stop for scheduled public transport vehicles. The sign may include a vehicle symbol, or the logo or name of the transport system or scheduled public transport operator. If the sign includes the inscription TAXI, it indicates a taxi stand for taxi vehicles marked with a roof light with the inscription TAXI. |
| IJ 4b | | Stop Sign This sign indicates a stop for a scheduled public transport vehicle. The sign may include the inscription ZASTÁVKA (STOP) or a bus or trolleybus symbol, the name of the stop, or the logo or name of the transport system or scheduled public transport operator. |
| IJ 4c | | Bus Stop This sign indicates the beginning of a bus or taxi stop. If the inscription TAXI is used instead of the bus symbol, the sign indicates the beginning of a taxi stand for taxi vehicles marked with a roof light with the inscription TAXI. |
| IJ 4d | | Tram Stop This sign indicates the beginning of a tram stop. To indicate a shared stop for a tram and a scheduled public transport vehicle or trolleybus, a single sign with corresponding symbols or a public transport vehicle symbol is used. |
| IJ 4e | | Trolleybus Stop This sign indicates the beginning of a trolleybus stop. To indicate a shared stop for a trolleybus and a scheduled public transport vehicle or tram, a single sign with corresponding symbols or a public transport vehicle symbol is used. |
| IJ 5 | | Information This sign indicates the nearest information center, or the direction or distance to it. The sign may also indicate operating hours. |
| IJ 6 | | Telephone This sign indicates the nearest public telephone station, or the direction or distance to it. |
| IJ 7 | | Filling Station This sign indicates the nearest fuel filling station, or the direction or distance to it. In the case of an alternative fuel filling station or electric vehicle charging station, the relevant designated symbol may be indicated on the sign. |
| IJ 8 | | Repair Shop This sign indicates the nearest motor vehicle repair shop, or the direction or distance to it. |
| IJ 9 | | Technical Inspection Station This sign indicates the nearest technical inspection station, or the direction or distance to it. |
| IJ 10 | | Hotel or Motel This sign indicates the nearest hotel or motel, or the direction or distance to it. |
| IJ 11a | | Restaurant This sign indicates the nearest restaurant, or the direction or distance to it. |
| IJ 11b | | Refreshments This sign indicates the nearest facility providing refreshments, or the direction or distance to it. |
| IJ 12 | | WC This sign indicates the nearest publicly accessible WC, or the direction or distance to it. |
| IJ 13 | | Rest Area This sign indicates the nearest suitable and equipped place for a short break and rest, or the direction or distance to it. |
| IJ 14a | | Campsite for Tents This sign indicates the nearest campsite for tents, or the direction or distance to it. |
| IJ 14b | | Campsite for Caravans This sign indicates the nearest campsite for caravans, or the direction or distance to it. |
| IJ 14c | | Campsite for Tents and Caravans This sign indicates the nearest campsite for tents and caravans, or the direction or distance to it. |
| IJ 15 | | Service Point for Sanitation of Recreational Vehicle Sanitary Facilities This sign indicates a service point for the sanitation of sanitary facilities of motorhomes and caravans. |
| IJ 16 | | Roadside Chapel This sign indicates the nearest place of prayer with parking, or the direction or distance to it. |
| IJ 17a | | Truckpark This sign usually informs on motorways and roads for motor vehicles about a comprehensive service facility for trucks, to which one turns at the nearest intersection. |
| IJ 17b | | Advance Direction Sign for Truckpark This sign informs about the distance to a comprehensive service facility for trucks. |
| IJ 18a | | Advance Direction Sign for Rest Area This sign informs about the distance to the nearest rest area and about the services provided at this rest area. The name of the rest area may be indicated on the sign. |
| IJ 18b | | Advance Direction Sign for Turn-off to Rest Area This sign usually informs about the beginning of the turning lane to the nearest rest area and about the services provided at this rest area. The name of the rest area may be indicated on the sign. |
| IJ 18c | | Directional Sign for Turn-off to Rest Area This sign informs about the turning point to the nearest rest area and about the services provided at this rest area. The name of the rest area may be indicated on the sign. |
| --- | --- | --- |

sprilohac6kvyhlas.text:
Supplementary Plates

| Number | Illustration | Name, Meaning and Use |
| E 1 | | Number The plate supplements the relevant sign, usually indicating danger or other information, with data on their number. |
| E 2a | | Intersection Shape The plate indicates the actual shape of the intersection. |
| E 2b | | Intersection Shape The plate indicates the actual shape of the intersection and specifies which road is main and which is secondary. |
| E 2c | | Intersection Shape The plate indicates the actual shape of the intersection and specifies which road is main and which is secondary. |
| E 2d | | Shape of Two Intersections The plate indicates the actual shape of two or more consecutive intersections and specifies which road is main and which is secondary. |
| E 3a | | Distance The plate indicates the distance to the place from which the sign, under which the plate is placed, is valid. |
| E 3b | | Distance The plate is used with the sign „Give Way!“ as a preliminary sign to „Stop, Give Way!“ and indicates the distance to the place where the obligation to stop the vehicle applies. |
| E 4 | | Length of Section The plate indicates the length of the section of validity of the sign, under which the plate is placed. |
| E 5 | | Maximum Permitted Mass The plate restricts the validity of a prohibition, restriction or command from the sign, under which it is placed, to a vehicle whose maximum permitted mass exceeds the value indicated on the plate. For a vehicle combination, the sum of the maximum permitted masses of all vehicles in the combination is decisive. |
| E 6 | | In Wet Conditions (in rain) The plate restricts the validity of the sign, under which it is placed, to wet road conditions. |
| E 7a | | Directional Arrow for Straight Ahead The plate indicates the direction to the place to which the sign, under which the plate is placed, relates. |
| E 7b | | Directional Arrow for Turning The plate indicates the direction to the place to which the sign, under which the plate is placed, relates. |
| E 8a | | Start of Section The plate indicates the start of the section of validity of the sign, under which the plate is placed. |
| E 8b | | Course of Section The plate indicates the course of the section of validity of the sign, under which the plate is placed. |
| E 8c | | End of Section The plate indicates the end of the section of validity of the sign, under which the plate is placed. |
| E 8d | | Section of Validity The sign indicates the section for which the sign, under which the plate is placed, is valid. |
| E 8e | | Section of Validity The sign indicates the section for which the sign, under which the plate is placed, is valid. |
| E9 | | Type of Vehicle The plate restricts the validity of the sign, under which it is placed, to the indicated type of vehicle. Relevant designated symbols are primarily used to indicate vehicle types. |
| E 10 | | Shape of Road-Railway Crossing The plate indicates the shape of the road-railway crossing and is used especially in the case of a sharp angle of this crossing. |
| E 11a | | Without Time-Based Toll The plate indicates a section of motorway whose use is not subject to a time-based toll. The plate can also be used to indicate a section of road whose use, unlike the preceding section, is not subject to a time-based toll. |
| E 11b | | With Time-Based Toll The plate indicates a section of road whose use is subject to a time-based toll. The plate can also be used to indicate a section of motorway whose use, unlike the preceding section, is subject to a time-based toll. |
| E 11c | | Without Toll The plate indicates a section of motorway whose use is not subject to a toll. The plate can also be used to indicate a section of road whose use, unlike the preceding section, is not subject to a toll. |
| E 11d | | With Toll The plate indicates a section of road whose use is subject to a toll. The plate can also be used to indicate a section of motorway whose use, unlike the preceding section, is subject to a toll. |
| E 11e | | Without Time-Based Toll and Toll The plate indicates a section of motorway whose use is not subject to a time-based toll or a toll. The plate can also be used to indicate a section of road whose use, unlike the preceding section, is not subject to a time-based toll or a toll. |
| E 11f | | With Time-Based Toll and Toll The plate indicates a section of road whose use is subject to a time-based toll and a toll. The plate can also be used to indicate a section of motorway whose use, unlike the preceding section, is subject to a time-based toll and a toll. |
| E 12a | | Cyclists Riding Against Traffic If placed under the sign „One-way traffic“, the plate informs drivers that cyclists are permitted to ride against traffic. The driver must adapt their behaviour to this fact, especially their obligation not to endanger other road users (cyclists). |
| E 12b | | Cyclists Entering Against Traffic Permitted If placed under the sign „No entry for all vehicles“, the plate permits cyclists to enter a one-way road against traffic. |
| E 12c | | Permitted Direction of Travel for Cyclists The plate, using appropriate directional arrows, indicates the possible direction of travel for cyclists. |
| E 13 | | Text or Symbol The meaning of the traffic sign, under which the supplementary plate is placed, is specified or restricted by an appropriate inscription or symbol on the supplementary plate. If the text on the supplementary plate uses the term „dopravní obsluha“ (traffic service), this refers to vehicles providing supply or medical, repair, maintenance, communal and similar services, vehicles transporting a severely disabled person, taxi vehicles, and vehicles whose drivers, operators or transported persons have their residence, accommodation, registered office or property in the areas beyond the sign. If the text on the supplementary plate uses the term „zásobování“ (supply), this refers to vehicles providing supply or medical, repair, maintenance, communal and similar services and vehicles transporting a severely disabled person. When placed under the traffic sign „Advance notice of rest area“ or „Advance notice of turn-off to rest area“ or „Notice for turn-off to rest area“, the plate may also include the designation of the operator of the filling station and catering facility operated at the rest area. |
| --- | --- | --- |
| | | |
| E 14 | | Transit The plate restricts the validity of the sign to goods vehicles for which the use of the section so marked is not essential for reaching the place of loading, unloading, maintenance or repair of the vehicle, the registered office, premises or usual residence of the carrier or driver. |
| E 15 | | Tunnel Category The plate supplements the sign „No entry for vehicles carrying dangerous goods“ and informs about the relevant tunnel category in the following section of the road. The category is indicated by letters „B“ to „E“ according to another legal regulation6). These signs are not used for tunnels of category „A“. |
| E 16 | | Distance to Next Filling Station The plate usually supplements the sign „Advance notice of rest area“ and informs about the distance to the filling station that follows the filling station located at the indicated rest area. |
| E 17 | | Insufficient Road Clearance Profile The plate, in combination with the traffic sign „Other danger“, warns of a restricted road clearance profile. |
| --- | --- | --- |

sprilohac8kvyhlas.text:
Horizontal traffic signs

- 1. Longitudinal lines

| Number | Illustration | Name, meaning and use |
| V 1a | | Continuous longitudinal line This sign is used primarily to separate traffic lanes with opposing traffic, to separate parts of merging lanes, and to separate parts of a turning or acceleration lane from a through lane. If the sign serves to separate traffic lanes with opposing traffic, the driver must drive to the right of this sign. It is forbidden to cross this sign or to extend a load over it, unless it is necessary for bypassing, overtaking a cyclist, turning off onto a place located outside the road, or entering the road from a place located outside the road. |
| V 1b | | Double continuous longitudinal line This sign is used to separate opposing traffic lanes on a road with more than two traffic lanes. In justified cases, the sign may also be used on a road with two traffic lanes. The driver is obliged to drive to the right of this sign. It is forbidden to cross this sign or to extend a load over it, unless it is necessary for bypassing. |
| V 2a | | Broken longitudinal line This sign is used to mark and separate traffic lanes. It is used to separate opposing traffic lanes in sections where sufficient visibility for overtaking is ensured and where there is no place requiring increased attention. The sign may be crossed in compliance with the relevant provisions arising from the general regulation of road traffic. The sign is made with gaps twice the length of the segments. |
| V 2b | | Broken longitudinal line This sign is used to mark and separate traffic lanes in a section where a broken line transitions into a continuous line, or to warn of a place requiring increased attention (bend, intersection, etc.). The sign may be crossed in compliance with the relevant provisions arising from the general regulation of road traffic. The sign is made with gaps of the same length or half the length of the segments. |
| V 2c | | Double broken longitudinal line This sign indicates a traffic lane used alternately for both directions of travel. If the traffic lane is not used in this way, this sign has the same meaning as a broken longitudinal line. |
| V 3 | | Continuous longitudinal line supplemented by a broken line If traffic lanes with opposing traffic are separated by this sign, only the meaning of the right line of this pair of lines applies to the driver. If traffic lanes in one direction of travel are indicated by this sign, the meaning of the closer line applies to the driver. |
| V 4 | | Guide line This sign indicates the edge of the roadway. |
| --- | --- | --- |

- 2. Transverse lines

| Number | Illustration | Name, meaning and use |
| V 5 | | Continuous transverse line This sign indicates the boundary of an intersection. It can be used to indicate a place for stopping a vehicle in the intersection area. |
| V 6a | | Continuous transverse line with the symbol „Give Way!“ This sign indicates the boundary of an intersection. It can be used to indicate a place for stopping a vehicle in the intersection area. |
| V 6b | | Continuous transverse line with the inscription STOP This sign indicates the boundary of an intersection. The sign indicates the place where the driver is obliged to stop the vehicle at the command of the sign „Stop, Give Way!“. |
| V 7a | | Pedestrian crossing This sign indicates a place designated for pedestrians to cross the road. |
| V 7b | | Crossing point This sign indicates a place adapted for pedestrians to cross the road. The driver of a vehicle must not endanger a pedestrian crossing the road at a crossing point. Before entering the roadway, the pedestrian must ascertain whether they can cross the roadway without endangering themselves or other road users. A pedestrian may cross the roadway only if, considering the distance and speed of approaching vehicles, they do not force their drivers to make a sudden change of direction or speed. |
| V 8a | | Cycle crossing This sign indicates an area designated for cyclists to cross the road. |
| V 8b | | Cycle crossing adjacent to a pedestrian crossing This sign indicates an area designated for cyclists to cross the road immediately adjacent to a pedestrian crossing. The area designated for cyclists to cross the road may be highlighted by a designated symbol or the sign „Cycle lane“. |
| V 8c | | Combined pedestrian and cycle crossing This sign indicates a pedestrian crossing combined with an area designated for cyclists to cross the road at the intersection of a pedestrian and cycle path with another road. |
| --- | --- | --- |

- 3. Arrows

| Number | Illustration | Name, meaning and use |
| V 9a | | Directional arrows The arrows indicate the method of lane assignment before an intersection or turning point and the prescribed direction of travel. The arrows may also be used to confirm an otherwise prescribed direction of travel. Temporary traffic arrangements on roads may be made only by the outline of this sign. |
| V 9b | | Advance arrows This sign indicates the approaching transition of a broken longitudinal line into a continuous line and the associated obligation for the driver to merge into the appropriate lane in time. |
| V 9c | | Advance arrows This sign indicates the approaching end of a traffic lane. The driver is obliged to merge into the through lane in time. |
| --- | --- | --- |

- 4. Marking of stopping and parking areas

| Number | Illustration | Name, meaning and use |
| V 10a | | Longitudinal parking This sign indicates permitted stopping and parking and, if applicable, specifies the method of parking in relation to the edge of the road. When parked, the vehicle must not, by its outline or load, extend into the marked area of the roadway. |
| V 10b | | Perpendicular parking This sign indicates permitted stopping and parking and, if applicable, specifies the method of parking in relation to the edge of the road. When parked, the vehicle must not, by its outline or load, extend into the marked area of the roadway. |
| V 10c | | Angled parking This sign indicates permitted stopping and parking and, if applicable, specifies the method of parking in relation to the edge of the road. When parked, the vehicle must not, by its outline or load, extend into the marked area of the roadway. |
| V 10d | | Parking lane This sign indicates permitted stopping and parking. When parked, the vehicle must not, by its outline or load, extend into the marked area of the roadway. |
| V 10e | | Reserved parking This sign indicates permitted stopping and parking and, if applicable, specifies the method of parking in relation to the edge of the road. When parked, the vehicle must not, by its outline or load, extend into the marked area of the roadway. The sign may indicate, with a suitable inscription, for whom the parking lot is reserved. |
| V 10f | | Reserved parking for a vehicle transporting a severely disabled person or a person with severe mobility impairment Only a vehicle marked with a parking permit indicating a vehicle transporting a severely disabled person may stop and park in the area marked by this sign. The sign always supplements the vertical traffic sign „Reserved parking“ with the symbol of a facility or area for persons in wheelchairs. |
| V 10g | | Restricted parking This sign indicates permitted stopping and parking and specifies the method of parking in relation to the edge of the road. When parked, the vehicle must not, by its outline or load, extend into the marked area of the roadway. In the section marked by this sign, parking is permitted under the conditions stipulated by the vertical traffic sign. |
| --- | --- | --- |

- 5. Marking of stops

| Number | Illustration | Name, meaning and use |
| V 11a | | Bus or trolleybus stop This sign indicates the area of a stop for designated public transport vehicles. If the inscription BUS is replaced by TAXI, it is a taxi stand designated for taxi vehicles marked with a roof light with the inscription TAXI. |
| V 11b | | Tram stop This sign indicates the area of a tram stop. |
| --- | --- | --- |

- 6. Marking of no stopping and no parking

| Number | Illustration | Name, meaning and use |
| V 12a | | Yellow zigzag line This sign indicates an area where parking is prohibited. When marking an area for delivery purposes, it may be supplemented with the inscription „ZAS“. |
| V 12b | | Yellow crossed lines This sign indicates an area which the driver must not enter if the traffic situation beyond this area is such that they would be forced to stop the vehicle in the marked area. |
| V 12c | | No stopping This sign is implemented as a continuous yellow line at the edge of the roadway and indicates a section where stopping and parking are prohibited. It may also be marked on the curb next to the roadway. |
| V 12d | | No parking This sign is implemented as a broken yellow line at the edge of the roadway and indicates a section where parking is prohibited. It may also be marked on the curb next to the roadway. |
| --- | --- | --- |

- 7. Other horizontal traffic signs

| Number | Illustration | Name, meaning and use |
| V 12e | | White zigzag line This sign provides advance warning of a place requiring increased caution. |
| V 13 | | Oblique parallel lines This sign indicates an area which it is forbidden to enter or to extend a load over, unless it is necessary for bypassing, turning off onto a place located outside the road, or entering the road from a place located outside the road. |
| V 14 | | Cycle lane This sign indicates a lane or path for cyclists. The bicycle symbol may, in justified cases, be supplemented by a smaller directional arrow or arrows. |
| V 15 | | Inscription on the roadway This sign expresses various supplementary information. Suitable symbols may also be used. |
| V 16 | | Safe distance Two arrows between two vehicles driving one after another indicate the minimum safe distance between vehicles on the given road at the maximum permitted speed under normal conditions. |
| V 17 | | Triangles This sign indicates an approach to an artificial unevenness on the roadway, e.g., a speed bump created by construction modification. |
| V 18 | | Optical psychological brake This sign optically, and possibly acoustically, guides drivers to reduce vehicle speed. The sign may also be marked in the form of a transversely interrupted line in the middle, with the gap between its two parts gradually shortening in the direction of travel. |
| V 19 | | Cyclist waiting area This sign indicates an area designated for cyclists waiting for the „Go“ traffic light signal. |
| V 20 | | Pictogram corridor for cyclists This sign indicates the area and direction of travel for cyclists. It warns drivers of motor vehicles that there is increased cyclist traffic on the road. |
| --- | --- | --- |

sprilohac9kvyhlas.text:
Light Signals

- 1. Light Signals

| Number | Illustration | Name, Meaning and Use |
| --- | --- | --- |
| S 1 |  | Three-colour system with full signals S 1a – Red light signal „Stop!“ S 1b – Yellow light signal „Caution!“ S 1c – Green light signal „Go“ For drivers turning right, only the signal located to the right of the lane designated for turning right or the signal located above this lane applies. |
| S 2 |  | Three-colour system with directional signals S 2a – Red light signal with directional arrow „Stop!“ S 2b – Yellow light signal with directional arrow „Caution!“ S 2c – Green light signal with directional arrow „Go“ |
| S 3 |  | Three-colour system with combined directional signals S 3a – Red light signal with combined directional arrow „Stop!“ S 3b – Yellow light signal with combined directional arrow „Caution!“ S 3c – Green light signal with combined directional arrow „Go“ |
| S 4a |  | Yellow light signal in the shape of a pedestrian |
| S 4b |  | Yellow light signal in the shape of a cyclist |
| S 4c |  | Yellow light signal in the shape of a pedestrian and cyclist |
| S 5 |  | Supplementary green arrow |
| S 6 |  | Signal for leaving an intersection |
| S 7 |  | Flashing yellow light The signal is in the shape of a full light. The signal may be supplemented by a symbol of a pedestrian, cyclist, or pedestrian and cyclist if it is necessary to specifically warn of crossing pedestrians or passing cyclists. The signal may also be used in the form of a light ramp of three to five lights placed horizontally side by side on a vehicle. When even and odd lights flash alternately, it warns of the need for increased caution; a progressive chain pointing to the left or right indicates to the driver the direction in which to bypass the obstacle. |
| S 8a |  | Prohibited entry of vehicles into the lane |
| S 8b |  | Free entry of vehicles into the lane |
| S 8c |  | Left light arrow |
| S 8d |  | Right light arrow |
| S 8e |  | Light cross The signal indicates an obstacle to road traffic next to the roadway. |
| S 9 |  | Two-colour system with pedestrian signals S 9a – Pedestrian signal with „Stop!“ sign S 9b – Pedestrian signal with „Go“ sign |
| S 10 |  | Three-colour system with cyclist signals S 10a – Cyclist signal with „Stop!“ sign S 10b – Cyclist signal with „Caution!“ sign S 10c – Cyclist signal with „Go“ sign |
| S 11 |  | Two-colour system with pedestrian and cyclist signals S 11a – Pedestrian and cyclist signal with „Stop!“ sign S 11b – Pedestrian and cyclist signal with „Go“ sign |
| S 12a |  | Speed signal with variable signal symbol |
| S 12b |  | Speed signal with multiple signal symbols |
| S 13 |  | Signal of two alternately flashing red lights placed side by side |
| S 14a |  | Two red alternately flashing lights of a level crossing warning device signal |
| S 14b |  | Flashing white light of a level crossing warning device signal |

- 2. Signals for trams (or for public transport buses or trolleybuses in lanes reserved for them)

| Number | Illustration | Types, Meaning and Use |
| S 15a |  | Stop If this signal regulates the operation of buses, scheduled passenger transport vehicles, and trolleybuses, this signal means for the drivers of these vehicles the obligation to stop before the intersection boundary or before the light signal. |
| S 15b |  | Go straight If this signal regulates the operation of buses, scheduled passenger transport vehicles, and trolleybuses, this signal means for the drivers of these vehicles the possibility to go straight. |
| S 15c |  | Turn left If this signal regulates the operation of buses, scheduled passenger transport vehicles, and trolleybuses, this signal means for the drivers of these vehicles the possibility to turn left. |
| S 15d |  | Turn right If this signal regulates the operation of buses, scheduled passenger transport vehicles, and trolleybuses, this signal means for the drivers of these vehicles the possibility to turn right. |
| S 15e |  | Go straight and right If this signal regulates the operation of buses, scheduled passenger transport vehicles, and trolleybuses, this signal means for the drivers of these vehicles the possibility to go straight or right. |
| S 15f |  | Go straight and left If this signal regulates the operation of buses, scheduled passenger transport vehicles, and trolleybuses, this signal means for the drivers of these vehicles the possibility to go straight or left. |
| S 15g |  | Turn right and left If this signal regulates the operation of buses, scheduled passenger transport vehicles, and trolleybuses, this signal means for the drivers of these vehicles the possibility to turn right or left. |
| --- | --- | --- |

sprilohac10kvyhla.text:
Traffic Devices

| Number | Illustration | Name, Meaning, and Use |
| Z 1 |  | Traffic Cone Traffic cones placed in a row, depending on their placement, serve as a continuous longitudinal line or a guide line, even if they are placed on another longitudinal line. They may also define an area into which entry is prohibited. |
| Z 2 |  | Barrier for Road Closure Marking The barrier is used primarily to mark a complete closure of a road or to mark the enclosure of a worksite. |
| Z 3 |  | Guide Board The guide board primarily indicates a dangerous curve where the driver must significantly reduce speed, and directs traffic in the direction of the arrows. If it is necessary to specifically draw attention to the curve, the white color may be replaced by yellow-green fluorescent color. The board may also be in the form of a single arrow or a series of individual arrows placed one after another. |
| Z 4a Z 4b |  | Directional Board with Diagonal Stripes Sloping Left Directional Board with Diagonal Stripes Sloping Right Directional boards with diagonal stripes direct traffic in the direction of the slope of the diagonal stripes. |
| Z 4c |  | Central Directional Board The central directional board directs traffic on both sides of the board. |
| Z 4d Z 4e |  | Directional Board with Arrow Left Directional Board with Arrow Right Directional boards with an arrow direct traffic in the direction of the arrow. |
| Z 5a Z 5b |  | Guide Board with Diagonal Stripes Sloping Left Guide Board with Diagonal Stripes Sloping Right Guide boards direct traffic in the direction of the slope of the diagonal stripes. |
| Z 5c |  | Central Guide Board with Diagonal Stripes The guide board directs traffic on both sides of the board. |
| Z 5d |  | Central Guide Board with Horizontal Stripes The guide board separates traffic lanes and serves as a continuous longitudinal line. |
| Z 6a Z 6b |  | Large Emphasizing Board Small Emphasizing Board Emphasizing boards emphasize or replace a continuous longitudinal line, typically of temporary horizontal traffic marking. |
| Z 7 |  | Mobile Road Closure Board The board is used primarily for short-term marking of an obstruction to traffic on roads, a lane closure, or a static or mobile worksite on a road. In its unchanged or folded form, the board is also used for approaching the marked location from a station and for returning to the station. |
| Z 8a |  | Stop Paddle for Police Officers |
| Z 8b |  | Stop Paddle for Other Persons |
| Z 8c |  | Directional Sign |
| Z 8d |  | Stop Paddle for Alternating Traffic The paddle is used to stop vehicles by a person designated for this purpose pursuant to § 79 para. 1 letter i) of the Act, especially during two-way traffic in a single lane during temporary traffic regulation on a road. The paddle is used such that the side with the symbol of the traffic sign „No Entry for All Vehicles“ faces the vehicles being signaled to stop, and the green side faces the vehicles that may continue driving. |
| Z 9 |  | Yellow and Black Stripes The stripes mark a permanent obstruction on the road. |
| Z 10 |  | Road Studs The studs supplement or emphasize horizontal traffic markings. Road studs supplementing a longitudinal line or a guide line are white. On bridges and sections with an increased risk of frequent black ice, blue studs may also be used. A series of yellow or orange road studs may be used as a substitute for a longitudinal line of temporary horizontal traffic marking. |
| Z 11a Z 11b |  | White Left Directional Post White Right Directional Post Posts (bollards) mark the edge of the road. |
| Z 11c Z 11d |  | Red Left Directional Post Red Right Directional Post Posts mark the exit of an access road onto another road. |
| Z 11e Z 11f |  | Blue Left Directional Post Blue Right Directional Post Posts mark a section of the road where there is an increased risk of icing. In a reduced version, they are also used on guardrails or bridge railings. |
| Z 11g |  | Red Round Directional Post The post can be used as a substitute for left and right red directional posts to mark the exit of an access road. |
| Z 11h |  | Green Round Directional Post – Baliseta The post is used for traffic guidance and to emphasize the continuous longitudinal line of horizontal traffic marking. |
| Z 12 |  | Short Transverse Hump The hump creates a short artificial unevenness on the roadway (speed hump) and is usually marked with yellow and black stripes. |
| Z 13 |  | Warning Hump The hump or series of humps creates an artificial unevenness warning of an approaching lane termination or an obstruction on the roadway or shoulder during temporary traffic regulation on a road, and if necessary, it can be driven over. |
| --- | --- | --- |

sprilohac11kvyhla.text:
Operational Information Devices

| Number | Illustration | Name, Meaning, and Use |
| ZPI 1 |  | Inscriptions The device serves to display information relevant to traffic on roads. Information is displayed using text or a suitable symbol. The device or part of it may also serve as a variable traffic sign. |
| ZPI 2 |  | Parking Lot Occupancy The device displays current information on whether there are free parking spaces in the parking lot (and possibly their number) or whether the parking lot is occupied or closed. |
| ZPI 3 |  | Thermometer The device typically provides information on road and air temperature. |
